title: Canvas Items, Tags, Layers, Rendering, and Redraw · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent accessibility club receives a different grid, seed, algorithm, input profile, display scale, assistance need, platform, and failure condition and transfers canvas items, tags, layers, rendering, and redraw into a changed tagged layered Canvas render plan. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of tagged layered Canvas render plan with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Distinguish retained Canvas items, integer IDs, tags, configuration, coordinates, stacking order, and deletion.
Define wall, terrain, solution, focus, annotation, and status layers with stable stacking rules.
Use semantic tags for groups and stable model keys rather than retaining fragile positional ID lists.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Canvas Item Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab puzzle team handles case 5290 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile retained render items, semantic tags, layer order, and redraw cleanup; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-render-evidence-g96gps" that uses case 5343 to reconcile retained render items, semantic tags, layer order, and redraw cleanup. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Render Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Render Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Render Evidence, keep Render Evidence connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-render-evidence-g96gps
# Competency: maze-render-evidence.
# Case 5343: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: all graph claims need an independent witness.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_render_evidence_g96gps(expected_items: int = 126, actual_items: int = 126, semantic_tags: int = 5, duplicate_items: int = 0, layer_errors: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_g96gps = "g96gps-spg69g-5343"
    assert evidence_variant_g96gps
    evidence_axes_g96gps = (
        "gridg96gps",
        "topology5343",
        "searchspg69g",
        "eventg96gps5343",
        "access5343g96gps",
        "recoveryspg69g5343",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_g96gps)) == 6
    if expected_items < 1 or actual_items < 0:
        return False, actual_items, "render-count"
    if actual_items != expected_items:
        return False, actual_items, "item-reconciliation"
    if semantic_tags < 4:
        return False, semantic_tags, "tag-contract"
    if duplicate_items > 0 or layer_errors > 0:
        return False, duplicate_items + layer_errors, "render-invariant"
    assert actual_items == expected_items
    return True, actual_items, "tagged-render-plan"
